answers: Vertical farming grows crops in stacked layers indoors using controlled environments, LED lighting, and hydroponic or aeroponic systems. It uses far less water and land than traditional farming.

Vertical farms can produce crops year-round regardless of weather, use 95% less water, no pesticides, and can be built in cities close to consumers, reducing transportation emissions.
Companies like AeroFarms, Plenty, and Bowery are leading the vertical farming industry. Leafy greens and herbs are the most common crops, though the technology is expanding to more varieties.
